$comment: Acceptance coverage ledger (#82, ADR-0001): every user-facing flow declares how it is covered — automated (playwright-e2e / storybook / unit-dom), manual (with a reason), or deferred (with an issue). check-e2e-coverage-map.mjs validates shape and path existence; check-acceptance-coverage-diff.mjs forces updates alongside renderer changes. Each epic seeds its own entries.

coverage.reason: OS-level reduced-motion toggling and subjective readability at 200% text scaling require the physical macOS/Windows acceptance pass documented in docs/acceptance/Acceptance-Test-Visual-Accessibility.md.

Follow docs/acceptance/Acceptance-Test-Context-Menus.md to verify native pointer placement, viewport-edge clamping, screen-reader announcement, and cross-surface focus restoration that automated lanes cannot fully observe.

A packaged macOS VoiceOver pass follows docs/acceptance/Acceptance-Test-Quick-Actions.md to verify modifier announcements, Command-click safety, app deactivation cleanup, and parity of pointer, keyboard, context-menu, and touch-visible access.

entries.$note: #689 rebuilt the macOS bar to the design-system six-menu spec (Overlook · File · Edit · View · Photo · Help). Structure/order/accelerators/target-aware enablement are asserted by application-menu.spec.ts (`…six-menu design-system spec…`) and application-menu-model.test.ts. The follow-up wired the new commands to their handlers — Toggle Sidebar and the Export parity path are exercised by application-menu.spec.ts (`…drive their shared handlers (parity)`), the sidebar reducer by app-state.test.ts; the router lives in use-native-command-router.ts and shares handlers with the toolbar/context menu (ADR-0024).

coverage.reason: A packaged macOS pass follows docs/acceptance/Acceptance-Test-Native-Application-Menu.md to verify native menu placement, inactive/hidden focus restoration, Services and OS roles, and assistive-technology behavior that the hidden Electron harness cannot observe.

Live provider credentials and the packaged signed iCloud host are owner-run closeout evidence in issue #337. Verify selection, album, lightbox, and settings entry points; exact review and custody counts; keyboard and narrow states; preserved selection/view/album; honest unavailable originals; and no backup namespace reuse.

The visible native-window 200K harness is intentionally outside per-PR CI; invoke npm run test:perf when the engine changes. It retains three trials per zoom in test-results/perf-report.json and gates the median drop rate plus maximum worst frame. CI keeps the 2,000-row variant.

docs/acceptance/acceptance-test-full-display-image.md covers the portrait-width/landscape-height Fill matrix, physical macOS trackpad inertia, image-first chrome, cross-aspect-ratio focal continuity, Inspector geometry, and reset boundaries that Playwright cannot reproduce faithfully.

Issue #499 visual parity uses the immutable 924x540 design/handoff/references/06-lightbox-default-contain.png source plus the measured HandoffTransformToolbar story and docs/acceptance/acceptance-test-full-display-image.md for side-by-side, Inspector, 200% zoom, focus-state, and reduced-motion review.

Run the signed macOS package against owner-supplied NEF, ARW, DNG, CR2, CR3, ORF, RW2, and RAF originals to validate Core Image camera support and confirm previews, repaired metadata, byte-faithful Original export, and zero persistent plaintext.

Follow docs/acceptance/Acceptance-Test-Appearance-Themes.md in a packaged macOS build to verify first-frame flash, native title-bar chrome, and live operating-system appearance changes that hidden automation cannot observe.

A real packaged pCloud token is OS-keychain-sealed and cannot be safely fabricated or reused in automation. The packaged pass verifies the confirmation and restart UI; the Electron E2E lane exercises the real profile custody files with the unpackaged-only insecure keystore.

Verify native screen-lock, suspend/resume, user-switch, idle, minimize/hide, macOS native full-screen transitions, password-manager behavior, VoiceOver, reduced motion, and minimum-window layout in packaged macOS and Windows builds; CI injects the lifecycle events but cannot reproduce the physical OS surfaces or native full-screen animation ordering.

On a notarized Developer ID build on a Touch ID Mac: upgrade a working com.qwts.overlook profile to com.zts1.overlook and prove existing Electron safeStorage custody still opens the library and provider tokens; verify the explicit password-confirmed Touch ID re-enrollment path, restart/unlock, cancel, failed scan, lockout, enrollment change, password fallback, password-change revocation, opt-out, and Keychain item accessibility/synchronization attributes. CI cannot provide both signed identities, physical biometric hardware, or mutate the enrolled biometric set.

Motion timings (120/200ms, easing) and hover fill progressions are visual-only rules with no cheap assertion surface; the disabled pattern (45% opacity + pointer-events) is exercised by component stories and every disabled-control play test, and copy/behavior rules are covered by the flow entries.

Native Finder pointer ownership, drag cancellation outside Chromium, and packaged NEF drops require a signed or unsigned macOS package; E2E covers capture-boundary routing, modal child churn, focus loss, mixed RAW/JPEG/HEIC paths, unsupported files, repeated drops, and window/navigation denial.

Use a packaged build configured with the owner's Google Desktop OAuth client to verify the real system-browser Picker callback, multi-selection, supported and unsupported file handling, and that the configured backup provider and account remain unchanged.
The #270/#577 CSS region contracts and toolbar actions are automated, but native OS drag interception cannot be synthesized honestly - owner verifies window movement in the packaged app.

The VoiceOver pass is the half axe cannot automate: announcement order, live-region behaviour, and whether a screen-reader user can actually complete each flow. Run on macOS with docs/acceptance/Manual-Test-A11y-VoiceOver.md whenever a child of #381 lands a semantics change.

entries.$note: Activity is reached only from Help → Activity… (#690), never a sidebar row; the Help-menu command and its dialog open are covered by application-menu.spec.ts, and Sidebar.stories.tsx regression-guards the row's absence.

coverage.reason: The signed provisioned macOS acceptance pass in docs/acceptance/Manual-Test-iCloud-Drive.md covers real Apple Account state changes, File Provider materialization, process restart, and fresh-profile disaster recovery that the deterministic Electron harness cannot reproduce.

docs/acceptance/acceptance-test-gif-webp-animated-media.md covers OS-level reduced-motion toggling, real animation timing/loop playback, and protected-album parity that the hidden Electron harness and Storybook cannot observe (prefers-reduced-motion is forced via a scoped matchMedia patch in stories).

The composed-board export at declared dimensions + color space (I4 raster / export-geometry E2E) is deferred to #696 pending the export owner's confirmation of the color-managed render path (spec section 09); transcode.ts currently strips ICC on the JPEG path. The board->output geometry is covered by the pure export-layout unit test.

Screen-reader traversal of the parallel reading-order list staying in sync with the role=application canvas, 200% text reflow of the panel and toolbar, and reduced-motion (no inertial/snap/drop animation) need the documented manual accessibility pass.

Follow the Titlebar Help Menu spec (§05/§08): on real Windows/Linux confirm no native menu bar renders, the titlebar Help button opens the ContextMenu with correct pointer placement and viewport-edge clamping across desktop-environment window-control layouts, RTL mirroring, reduced-motion, 200% text scaling, and native menu/menuitem screen-reader semantics that automated lanes cannot fully observe.
